deep_dive.title: MKGR: Multimodal Knowledge-Graph Representation Learning for Cold-Start Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: Accurate protein-protein interaction (PPI) prediction is central to functional genomics, disease mechanism discovery, and drug development. A difficult setting arises when candidate interactions include proteins that have no observed PPI edges during training, where models relying on network topology alone often lose useful context. This paper presents \method, a multimodal representation framework for cold-start PPI prediction. \method\ combines region-aware protein sequence encoding with four protein-centered biomedical knowledge graphs, including protein-drug, protein-disease, protein-miRNA, and protein-lncRNA associations. The sequence branch extracts contextual representations from structurally informed sequence regions, while graph attention encoders learn modality-specific protein embeddings from sparse biomedical associations. A bridge reconstruction objective regularizes graph learning by recovering shared protein-entity associations, and a pair-level gating module adaptively integrates sequence and graph evidence for each candidate protein pair. Experiments on two benchmark datasets under novel-old and novel-novel cold-start settings show that \method\ consistently outperforms competitive sequence, network, and knowledge-graph baselines across ACC, F1, AUC, AUPR, and MCC.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: cold-start interaction prediction using multimodal knowledge graphs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用多模态知识图谱进行冷启动相互作用预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用多模态知识图谱进行冷启动相互作用预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用多模态知识图谱进行冷启动相互作用预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MKGR combines region-aware protein sequence encoding with four biomedical knowledge graphs (including protein-drug) for cold-start protein-protein interaction prediction, demonstrating strong transferability to drug-related tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对冷启动场景下蛋白质相互作用预测中缺乏网络拓扑信息的问题，本文提出MKGR多模态框架，融合区域感知序列编码与四种生物医学知识图谱（包含蛋白-药物关联），在无历史交互边的情况下实现精准预测。实验表明，该方法在多个基准上显著优于纯序列或纯拓扑模型，为药物发现中冷启动相互作用预测提供了可迁移的方法论。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对冷启动场景下蛋白质相互作用预测中缺乏网络拓扑信息的问题，本文提出MKGR多模态框架，融合区域感知序列编码与四种生物医学知识图谱（包含蛋白-药物关联），在无历史交互边的情况下实现精准预测。实验表明，该方法在多个基准上显著优于纯序列或纯拓扑模型，为药物发现中冷启动相互作用预测提供了可迁移的方法论。

quick_skim.title: Integrating Semantic Retrieval, LLM-based Refinement, and Structured Expert Curation for Scalable AOP Gene Mapping
quick_skim.abstract: Toxicogenomics can support regulatory toxicology, but its use is limited by the difficulty of translating molecular responses into mechanistic, decision-relevant interpretations. Adverse Outcome Pathways (AOPs) provide a framework for this translation, yet omics applications require scalable mapping of Key Events (KEs) to molecular features. Here, we present an AI-assisted, multi-step workflow for KE-to-gene mapping that uses embedding-based semantic retrieval to identify candidate ontology/pathway terms, large language model-assisted refinement to filter these candidates, and double-independent expert group curation with rule-based consolidation to finalize mappings and derive confidence scores. Compared with earlier NLP-based approaches, the workflow improves KE-to-ontology/pathway mapping performance and generates candidate annotations that better align with expert judgment while substantially reducing the need for manual augmentation. Explicit gene and protein mentions in KE titles were additionally grounded to improve specificity, and each curated mapping was assigned curator reason codes to support transparent, traceable, and confidence-aware reuse. Applied across AOP-Wiki, the workflow produced a comprehensive KE-to-gene set resource covering 1,254 KEs across 523 AOPs and linking 15,833 human genes. Utility is demonstrated through CTD-based AOP fingerprinting of curated reference chemical groups, highlighting expanded coverage and confidence-informed interpretation of chemical-associated gene signatures in an AOP context. The workflow and resulting resource provide a practical bridge between toxicogenomics and AOP-based mechanistic interpretation and support routine updating and future extension to additional omics layers within OECD Omics2AOP.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: integrates LLM refinement with semantic retrieval for biomedical mapping
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 整合LLM细化和语义检索进行生物医学映射
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 整合LLM细化和语义检索进行生物医学映射
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 整合LLM细化和语义检索进行生物医学映射
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: An AI workflow using LLM-assisted refinement and semantic retrieval for KE-to-gene mapping in AOPs. Methodologically bridges LLM+KG for biomedical entity linking, relevant to cold-start DDI.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一种AI辅助工作流，结合嵌入语义检索、大语言模型细化和专家策展，实现不良结局路径中关键事件到基因的可扩展映射。工作流展示了LLM与知识图谱结合在生物医学实体关联中的有效性，其方法框架可直接应用于冷启动药物相互作用预测中的药物-药物关系发现，尤其是对于新药缺乏已知相互作用的情况。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一种AI辅助工作流，结合嵌入语义检索、大语言模型细化和专家策展，实现不良结局路径中关键事件到基因的可扩展映射。工作流展示了LLM与知识图谱结合在生物医学实体关联中的有效性，其方法框架可直接应用于冷启动药物相互作用预测中的药物-药物关系发现，尤其是对于新药缺乏已知相互作用的情况。
